Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks bills to locate the KeyMove vendor bill. The agent uses quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll true to retrieve all bills. Among the 17 bills in the system the agent finds bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 with DocNumber KM-44192-ICR, TotalAmt 1200, Balance 1200, from vendor KeyMove Specialty Transport (VendorRef VEND-KEYMOVE-001). The bill line description reads Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction, charged to Claims and Remediation Expense (ACC-6185). Transaction date April 17, due date April 24, which is 2 days past due as of April 26. The full balance remaining confirms the bill has not been paid.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks invoices to find the NorthWind invoice INV-2026-0412. The agent uses quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria referencing DocNumber INV-2026-0412. The invoice (Id inv_northwind_denver_02) totals 14700 with 6 line items: Line 1 Raj Patel Standard Relocation 4500, Line 2 Hazmat Handling Surcharge for lithium UPS batteries 1800, Line 3 Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol 1200, Line 4 Emilia Cruz Standard Relocation 4500, Line 5 Specialty Piano Moving for Steinway Model B 2200, and Line 6 Early Lease Termination Negotiation 500. Billed to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, due May 1. Line 5 at 2200 is the piano moving amount Hana referenced in the prompt.
OE 3: Search QuickBooks vendors to verify the KeyMove vendor record and compare against known vendors. The agent uses quickbooks_search_vendors with fetchAll true. The results include VEND-KEYMOVE-001 (KeyMove Specialty Transport) alongside the established vendors Hana normally works with: Heartland Movers, Swift Relocations, and Atlas Corporate Travel. KeyMove being present in QuickBooks but absent from Hana's usual vendor list confirms it was added without standard Finance onboarding.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for KeyMove to trace internal discussion about this vendor. The agent uses conversations_search_messages with search_query KeyMove. The results include approximately 4 messages: Marcus Thorne in channel C005 (#finance) noting the KeyMove bill creates a margin erosion pattern on NorthWind, Blessing Okafor in channel C006 (#operations) taking responsibility for the piano damage and confirming she green-lit the extraction plan off a thin assessment without requiring a pre-move site survey, and references to Craig Nguyen's damage photos. These messages reveal Blessing Okafor engaged KeyMove as the piano subcontractor.
OE 5: Search Slack messages for piano or Steinway to gather the full damage context. The agent uses conversations_search_messages with search_query piano (or Steinway or Emilia Cruz piano). The results include approximately 11 messages: Julian Brooks in channel C007 (#support) logging that Emilia Cruz called upset on April 12 asking whether MoveOps would take responsibility for the scratch, Hana Kim's own message in C005 (#finance) posting the INV-2026-0412 breakdown where the 2200 piano line was noted as PROVISIONAL and based on a Gentle Giant quote, Chloe Vance in C006 (#operations) explaining that neither Heartland nor Swift has piano specialists on their crews, and Catalina Dubois posting the NorthWind account update in C006 mentioning the Steinway as a specialty move complication. Hana's own message reveals the original piano vendor was supposed to be Gentle Giant Piano Movers, not KeyMove.
OE 6: Search emails for Craig Nguyen to find the KeyMove damage correspondence. The agent uses search_emails with query Craig Nguyen (or KeyMove). The results include an email from craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com (CC claims@keymove-specialty.com) to blessing.okafor@moveops.com containing damage photos and extraction notes. The email documents a surface scratch approximately 4.5 to 5 inches on the bass side rim near the keyboard cheek block of Emilia Cruz's Steinway Model B grand piano. Damage occurred during extraction at the second-floor landing on April 11 when the piano had to be rotated more aggressively than planned to clear the interior railing. This confirms Blessing was the MoveOps operational contact who worked directly with KeyMove on site.
OE 7: Search emails for Emilia Cruz to gather broader context on the piano move and NorthWind relationship. The agent uses search_emails with query Emilia Cruz. The results include approximately 4 emails: Pam Kowalski at NorthWind providing Emilia's Chicago lease details (Lincoln Property Company, 1455 S Michigan Ave, lease through May 15, 5700 early termination penalty), Catalina Dubois emailing David Chen about the NorthWind Denver expansion including the piano complications, Catalina's service recovery commitment email to Pam Kowalski promising a comprehensive plan within the week, and Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning escalation to David Chen citing the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and the Emilia Cruz piano damage as the two incidents driving NorthWind's evaluation of alternative vendors.
OE 8: Search Slack messages for Gentle Giant to trace the original vendor plan and understand when the switch happened. The agent uses conversations_search_messages with search_query Gentle Giant. The results include approximately 3 messages confirming Gentle Giant Piano Movers (Chicago) was the originally recommended piano subcontractor per Chloe Vance's operational assessment, that Gentle Giant had no existing vendor agreement with MoveOps, and that Hana's invoice was priced using a Gentle Giant preliminary quote. No documentation exists in Slack of when or why the vendor was switched from Gentle Giant to KeyMove. The switch appears to have happened at the coordinator level without formal authorization.
OE 9: Search CRM for the NorthWind company record to get the company_id for further lookups. The agent uses crm_search_companies with name NorthWind. The result returns company_northwind (NorthWind Technologies). This company_id is needed for subsequent CRM deal and engagement searches.
OE 10: Search CRM deals for NorthWind to understand the financial context of the account. The agent uses crm_search_deals with company_id company_northwind. The results include multiple deals: deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (14700, contractSent stage, 2 relocations for Denver satellite office expansion) and the original deal_northwind_won (60000, closedWon, 5 enterprise relocations at 12000 each). The Denver expansion deal at 14700 for 2 moves averages 7350 per move, well below the original 12000 per move enterprise rate, confirming a pricing gap that compounds the margin concern.
OE 11: List CRM engagements for NorthWind to find account activity notes and support history. The agent uses crm_list_engagements with company_ids containing company_northwind. The results include eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context with detailed account recovery background: NorthWind gave a 5 out of 10 CSAT score in Q1 after apartment quality failures and slow response times, Pam Kowalski escalated to David Chen, and the Denver expansion represents a second-chance opportunity. Also finds a logged support call (Julian Brooks) from Emilia Cruz on April 12 where she asked directly whether MoveOps was taking responsibility for the piano damage. These engagements reveal the account is in critical retention mode.
OE 12: Search Airtable for Emilia Cruz's relocation record to verify the vendor assignment and move details. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Emilia Cruz. The result includes record recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver: NorthWind Technologies, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Denver, Move Start Date April 14, Assigned Coordinator Blessing Okafor. Special requirements list the Steinway Model B grand piano (7ft, 480 lbs) and note Gentle Giant Piano Movers (Chicago) as the potential subcontractor with no existing vendor agreement. Three-vendor coordination was planned: Heartland for household goods, piano subcontractor TBD, and apartment sourcing. This record confirms the original plan named Gentle Giant as the piano vendor, not KeyMove.
OE 13: Search Airtable for Raj Patel's relocation record to understand the second NorthWind move. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Raj Patel. The result includes record recRajPatelChicagoDenver: NorthWind Technologies, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Denver, Move Start Date April 21, Assigned Coordinator Blessing Okafor. Special requirements detail the home server lab with 4 rack-mounted servers (approximately 2400 lbs), lithium UPS batteries requiring Class 9 hazmat handling, and CISO-mandated chain-of-custody protocol. The agent may also find recRajPatelAuditNote0411 documenting a chain-of-custody breach: movers arrived at 7 AM and loaded the servers before the IT security escort confirmed for 9 AM arrived. This reveals a second service failure on the NorthWind account beyond the piano damage.
OE 14: Search Linear for NorthWind-related issues to find the tracking ticket the prompt asks to update. The agent uses linear_list_issues with query NorthWind. The results include linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 titled NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations, number 482, priority 1 (urgent), assigned to David Chen (moveops_david_chen), due date April 24 which is 2 days past due as of April 26. The issue description references Pam Kowalski's formal escalation, Victor Huang's security breach email, Chloe Vance's ops timeline, Julian Brooks's support note, Catalina's recovery plan commitment, and Alejandro Fuentes's draft retention pricing model. This is the tracking ticket the agent should update with the finance findings. The agent also finds issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (DOT Docs for Raj Patel lithium UPS, state Done, assigned to Chloe Vance).
OE 15: Search emails for Greg Pallone or Swift pricing to find vendor quotes for surcharge cross-referencing. The agent uses search_emails with query Greg Pallone (or Swift surcharge or DOT documentation). The result includes an email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com dated April 11 announcing a 15 percent base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings and a 500 dollar per-shipment DOT documentation surcharge effective immediately. The email lists 4 in-progress MoveOps shipments subject to the surcharge including Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz, though the routes listed for both employees (Dallas TX to Denver CO for Raj, Phoenix AZ to Portland OR for Emilia) do not match their actual Chicago-to-Denver routes in Airtable. This discrepancy is a potential confusion point. The surcharges have not yet been billed by Swift and represent unbooked vendor costs.
OE 16: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's prior review of the KeyMove bill to understand what Marcus already knows. The agent uses search_emails with query KeyMove bill (or Marcus KeyMove, or searches broadly). The result includes an email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com) where Marcus states he reviewed the KeyMove bill, confirms the paperwork lines up with Craig Nguyen's April 11 damage email and Blessing's note that the walkup assessment was insufficient, and recommends processing it unless someone has a basis to dispute. Marcus also flags that the pattern of service failures on NorthWind is turning into both a margin problem and a retention problem. Hana Kim was not CC'd on this email. This reveals Marcus already knows about the bill and has a preliminary recommendation, which the agent should account for when drafting the email to Marcus.
OE 17: Search contacts for Marcus to get his email address for the required email. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Marcus. Disambiguation trap: the search returns 3 contacts named Marcus. Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com, Head of Finance at MoveOps) is the correct target since he is Hana's manager. The other two are Marcus Webb at Ironclad Cybersecurity (m.webb@ironcladsec.com) and Marcus Webb at BrightLoop Analytics (marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com). The agent must select Marcus Thorne as the recipient.
OE 18: List Slack channels to identify the channel ID for #finance. The agent uses channels_list with channel_types public_channel. The results return all public channels. The #finance channel is C005 (name finance, purpose Budgeting invoicing vendor payments and margin analysis). The agent needs this channel ID to post findings in #finance as the prompt requests.
OE 19 (Write Action): Add a comment to the NorthWind retention tracking ticket in Linear. The agent uses linear_create_comment with issueId linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 and body containing the financial findings: the KeyMove unapproved vendor spend of 1200 for the insurance claim rider with no PO or vendor agreement, the vendor switch from Gentle Giant to KeyMove by Blessing Okafor, the connection to INV-2026-0412 Line 5 where 2200 was billed PROVISIONAL based on a Gentle Giant quote, unbilled costs still outstanding (KeyMove base transport fee unknown amount, Swift DOT surcharges, piano tuning at Denver), and margin impact analysis on the NorthWind account. The agent could alternatively use linear_update_issue to append to the description, but linear_create_comment is the safer approach since it adds to the ticket without overwriting existing content.
OE 20 (Write Action): Post investigation findings in the #finance Slack channel. The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C005 and payload containing: the unapproved vendor spend alert for KeyMove Specialty Transport, who authorized the vendor engagement (Blessing Okafor as coordinator, switching from Gentle Giant without Finance approval), how the 1200 insurance claim rider connects to the 2200 piano line on INV-2026-0412, the list of unbilled costs across both NorthWind moves (KeyMove base piano transport, Swift DOT surcharges, piano tuning, chain-of-custody actual cost unconfirmed), the surcharge cross-reference showing actual vendor costs trending above what was invoiced to NorthWind, and recommended actions.
OE 21 (Write Action): Send email to Marcus Thorne about the unapproved vendor spend. The agent uses send_email with sender hana.kim@moveops.com, recipients containing marcus.thorne@moveops.com, subject referencing KeyMove unapproved vendor spend or NorthWind Emilia Cruz, and content covering: who brought KeyMove in (Blessing Okafor authorized the vendor without Finance sign-off), how the piano claim connects to what was billed NorthWind (Line 5 at 2200 was PROVISIONAL based on Gentle Giant, the 1200 KeyMove rider is additional cost, and the base transport bill has not arrived yet), other unbilled costs on the two NorthWind moves, surcharge cross-reference against vendor quotes showing margin erosion, and recommended actions including whether to hold or process the past-due KeyMove bill.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search QuickBooks bills to locate the KeyMove vendor bill. The agent uses quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll true to retrieve all bills. OE 15: Search emails for Greg Pallone or Swift pricing to find vendor quotes for surcharge cross-referencing. The agent uses search_emails with query Greg Pallone (or Swift surcharge or DOT documentation). The result includes an email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com dated April 11 announcing a 15 percent base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings and a 500 dollar per-shipment DOT documentation surcharge effective immediately. The email lists 4 in-progress MoveOps shipments subject to the surcharge including Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz, though the routes listed for both employees (Dallas TX to Denver CO for Raj, Phoenix AZ to Portland OR for Emilia) do not match their actual Chicago-to-Denver routes in Airtable. This discrepancy is a potential confusion point. The surcharges have not yet been billed by Swift and represent unbooked vendor costs.
OE 16: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's prior review of the KeyMove bill to understand what Marcus already knows. The agent uses search_emails with query KeyMove bill (or Marcus KeyMove, or searches broadly). The result includes an email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com) where Marcus states he reviewed the KeyMove bill, confirms the paperwork lines up with Craig Nguyen's April 11 damage email and Blessing's note that the walkup assessment was insufficient, and recommends processing it unless someone has a basis to dispute. Marcus also flags that the pattern of service failures on NorthWind is turning into both a margin problem and a retention problem. Hana Kim was not CC'd on this email. This reveals Marcus already knows about the bill and has a preliminary recommendation, which the agent should account for when drafting the email to Marcus.
OE 17: Search contacts for Marcus to get his email address for the required email. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Marcus. Disambiguation trap: the search returns 3 contacts named Marcus. Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com, Head of Finance at MoveOps) is the correct target since he is Hana's manager. The other two are Marcus Webb at Ironclad Cybersecurity (m.webb@ironcladsec.com) and Marcus Webb at BrightLoop Analytics (marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com). The agent must select Marcus Thorne as the recipient.
OE 18: List Slack channels to identify the channel ID for #finance. The agent uses channels_list with channel_types public_channel. The results return all public channels. The #finance channel is C005 (name finance, purpose Budgeting invoicing vendor payments and margin analysis). The agent needs this channel ID to post findings in #finance as the prompt requests.
OE 19 (Write Action): Add a comment to the NorthWind retention tracking ticket in Linear. The agent uses linear_create_comment with issueId linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 and body containing the financial findings: the KeyMove unapproved vendor spend of 1200 for the insurance claim rider with no PO or vendor agreement, the vendor switch from Gentle Giant to KeyMove by Blessing Okafor, the connection to INV-2026-0412 Line 5 where 2200 was billed PROVISIONAL based on a Gentle Giant quote, unbilled costs still outstanding (KeyMove base transport fee unknown amount, Swift DOT surcharges, piano tuning at Denver), and margin impact analysis on the NorthWind account. The agent could alternatively use linear_update_issue to append to the description, but linear_create_comment is the safer approach since it adds to the ticket without overwriting existing content.
OE 20 (Write Action): Post investigation findings in the #finance Slack channel. The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C005 and payload containing: the unapproved vendor spend alert for KeyMove Specialty Transport, who authorized the vendor engagement (Blessing Okafor as coordinator, switching from Gentle Giant without Finance approval), how the 1200 insurance claim rider connects to the 2200 piano line on INV-2026-0412, the list of unbilled costs across both NorthWind moves (KeyMove base piano transport, Swift DOT surcharges, piano tuning, chain-of-custody actual cost unconfirmed), the surcharge cross-reference showing actual vendor costs trending above what was invoiced to NorthWind, and recommended actions.
OE 21 (Write Action): Send email to Marcus Thorne about the unapproved vendor spend. The agent uses send_email with sender hana.kim@moveops.com, recipients containing marcus.thorne@moveops.com, subject referencing KeyMove unapproved vendor spend or NorthWind Emilia Cruz, and content covering: who brought KeyMove in (Blessing Okafor authorized the vendor without Finance sign-off), how the piano claim connects to what was billed NorthWind (Line 5 at 2200 was PROVISIONAL based on Gentle Giant, the 1200 KeyMove rider is additional cost, and the base transport bill has not arrived yet), other unbilled costs on the two NorthWind moves, surcharge cross-reference against vendor quotes showing margin erosion, and recommended actions including whether to hold or process the past-due KeyMove bill.
